memberdef.type: PACKAGE_SCOPE const TSharedRef< const FUniqueNetId > const TSharedRef< FUserOnlineAccount > &
memberdef.definition: PACKAGE_SCOPE const TSharedRef<const FUniqueNetId> const TSharedRef<FUserOnlineAccount>& FOnlineIdentityAccelByte::Account
memberdef.name: Account

detaileddescription.para: Parent subsystem that spawned this instance
Simple mapping for LocalUserNum to FUniqueNetIdAccelByte for users. Filled when users log in.
Mapping of local user indices to login statuses, used to query login status for a user
Simple mapping for FUniqueNetIdAccelByte to LocalUserNum. Filled when users log in.
Mapping of AccelByte net IDs to AccelByte user accounts. Filled when users log in.
Flag denoting whether or not we are currently authenticating as a server to prevent duplicate logins
Flag denoting whether or not we have successfully authenticated as a server in this instance
Flag denoting whether or not we create headless account on successfully 3rd party login using AutoLogin
Array of pending server login delegates, will be all fired once login is finished
Construct an instance of our identity interface

InSubsystem
Subsystem that owns this identity interface
Begin FOnlineIdentityAccelByte

memberdef.argsstring: (const FUniqueNetId &UserId, EUserPrivileges::Type Privilege, const FOnGetUserPrivilegeCompleteDelegate &Delegate) override
memberdef.name: GetUserPrivilege

detaileddescription.para: Extra method to try and get a LocalUserNum from a FUniqueNetId instance
Get the FApiClient that is used for a particular user by their net ID.
Used to make raw SDK calls for user if needed.
Get the FApiClient that is used for a particular user by their net ID.
Used to make raw SDK calls for user if needed.
Authenticate a server on the backend using the FRegistry global API client

#TODO (Maxwell): This really should be supported by AutoLogin as a separate authentication path, as there's a ton of hacky code we need to write to make sure that any server call is working when authenticated. If we use AutoLogin for server auth, we bypass a lot of that code. However, this would require a rework to the current server code that we don't have time for... For now, use this method for all server auth...
Check whether or not our server is authenticated or not

memberdef.argsstring: (MAX_LOCAL_PLAYERS, OnConnectLobbyComplete, bool, const FUniqueNetId &, const FString &)
memberdef.name: DEFINE_ONLINE_PLAYER_DELEGATE_THREE_PARAM

memberdef.argsstring: (MAX_LOCAL_PLAYERS, OnLoginWithOAuthErrorComplete, bool, const FUniqueNetId &, const FErrorOAuthInfo &)
memberdef.name: DEFINE_ONLINE_PLAYER_DELEGATE_THREE_PARAM

memberdef.argsstring: (MAX_LOCAL_PLAYERS, AccelByteOnLoginComplete, bool, const FUniqueNetId &, const FOnlineErrorAccelByte &)
memberdef.name: DEFINE_ONLINE_PLAYER_DELEGATE_THREE_PARAM

detaileddescription.para: Called when user account login has completed after calling Login() or AutoLogin()

parameterdescription.para: the controller number of the associated user
true if server was contacted and a valid result received
the user id received from the server on successful login

detaileddescription.para: Delegate used in notifying the that manual logout completed

parameterdescription.para: the controller number of the associated user
whether the async call completed properly or not

memberdef.argsstring: (MAX_LOCAL_PLAYERS, AccelByteOnConnectLobbyComplete, bool, const FUniqueNetId &, const FOnlineErrorAccelByte &)
memberdef.name: DEFINE_ONLINE_PLAYER_DELEGATE_THREE_PARAM

detaileddescription.para: Called when user account login succesfully connected to lobby

parameterdescription.para: the controller number of the associated user
true if server was contacted and a valid result received
the user id received from the server on successful connect

detaileddescription.para: Set whether to create headless account when try to do 3rd party login using AutoLogin. The default value is true.

briefdescription.para: This function generate ab code that can be exchanged into publisher namespace token (i.e. by web portal).

detaileddescription.para: Set login status.
Add a new authenticated server to the identity interface mappings for V2 session flow
Convenience method to get an instance of this interface from the subsystem passed in.

parameterdescription.para: Subsystem instance that we wish to get this interface from
Instance of the interface that we got from the subsystem, or nullptr if not found

simplesect.para: boolean that is true if we could get an instance of the interface, false otherwise

detaileddescription.para: Convenience method to get an instance of this interface from the subsystem associated with the world passed in.

parameterdescription.para: World instance that we wish to get the interface from
Instance of the interface that we got from the subsystem, or nullptr if not found

simplesect.para: boolean that is true if we could get an instance of the interface, false otherwise

detaileddescription.para: To decide whether the current user should be logged out or not. Logout required for specific range of codes. Which means connection closure is abnormal and client should not reconnect (i.e. ban & access token revocation)
Disable the default constructor, as we only want to be able to construct this interface passing in the parent subsystem
Call when the user logged out.

parameterdescription.para: Index of the user who logged out
True if the user is successfully logged out or False otherwise.

detaileddescription.para: Remove all mappings for a user by their user index. Should only be called when the user logs out.

parameterdescription.para: Index of the user that we wish to remove mappings for

memberdef.argsstring: (const FUniqueNetId &UserId, EUserPrivileges::Type Privilege, uint32 PrivilegeResults, FOnGetUserPrivilegeCompleteDelegate OriginalDelegate, TSharedRef< const FUniqueNetIdAccelByteUser > AccelByteId)
memberdef.name: OnGetNativeUserPrivilegeComplete

detaileddescription.para: Handler for when our internal call to the native platform for querying user privileges completes.
Handler for when we finish authenticating a server to fire off delegates
Handler for when we fail to authenticate a server to fire off delegates
AccelByte service implementation of the online identity interface
